Upon request to archive@schoolofmusic.ucla.edu digitized works can be removed from public view if there are rights issues that need to be resolved.
Photograph of Sam Browne (top row, far right) with the Boys' Glee Club of Jefferson High School, located in Los Angeles, California. Sam Browne, along with social studies teachers Hazel Gottschalk Whitaker and Marjorie Bright, would officially break the color barrier for Black teachers in the secondary schools of Los Angeles Unified in 1936. Browne convinced the Los Angeles Board of Education to allow jazz to be taught. He instructed and mentored some of the greatest names in jazz including Chico Hamilton, O.C. Smith, Dexter Gordon, Buddy Collette, and Art Farmer. He also took his students to the jazz clubs on Central Ave., and orchestra rehearsals by Duke Ellington and Lionel Hampton. Originally on page 20 of AMRA 0001.
